summary: Iteration 6 — backend-only testing of v4.2 Trading Station detail endpoints, two-phase Royale elimination engine, tournament journey + bracket endpoints, and regression of v4.1 matchmaking. Added /app/backend/tests/test_journey_and_royale.py with 25 pytest cases (23 passed, 2 conditionally skipped — fallback user happened to be a participant of the lobby/tournament being tested, which the test gracefully skips). Combined with the existing 40-case suite, total 63 passed / 2 skipped, zero failures. All new endpoints behave exactly per spec.

minor.issue: When duel.status == 'completed' / 'cancelled' the equity_series for me/opponent is returned as []; UI must handle the empty-series case. compute_duel_pnl is only called for status=='live'; for any other status the response uses final_pnl_a/b (which may be 0 for cancelled duels). Cosmetic — not a bug, but worth flagging for the FE.

The seed creates lobbies with absolute started_at timestamps. As the backend stays up, the seeded '22m in' lobby drifts past phase 2 into 'finished' (~34m elapsed after ~12m uptime). For demo/UX, consider re-seeding live lobbies on app startup OR using a relative-to-now started_at recomputed on each backend boot. Tests now force started_at via direct mongo update to exercise the phase math deterministically.

lobby_specs declares size=20 for one live lobby but only 10 users are seeded, so participant_ids has only 10. total_active will never reach 20. Acceptable for demo, but if the spec needs a true 20-trader royale, more bot users need to be seeded.

action_items: (Optional) On backend startup, refresh started_at for live royale lobbies relative to current 'now' to keep the seeded phase-1/phase-2 demo accurate across long-running containers.
(Optional) Seed more bot users so the 20-trader / 50-trader royale lobbies actually fill to their declared size (currently capped to 10 real users).
(Carry-over from iter 5) Split server.py (~1940 lines) into routers; remove duplicate app.include_router calls.
critical_code_review_comments: simulator.compute_royale_state: when status is 'live' but started_at is None it returns 'pre' silently; consider warning so misconfigured lobbies don't pass UI checks.

server.py:_build_journey — when bracket data is missing for a user in a 'Completed' tournament (e.g., user advanced from groups but never appears in R16), exit_label falls back to last path stage which may be misleading; consider explicit 'No bracket entry' label.

server.py:_prize_for_exit — Champion case uses int(prize_pool * 0.15) only; the table for 'Final' (runner-up) sums to a much larger amount than the champion. Verify this matches the prize_distribution shown to users: Champion 15%, Runner-up 15%, SF 20%/4, QF 25%/8, R16 25%/16. Looks correct on inspection, but runner-up + champion both get 15% which is unusual — confirm with product.
server.py:1652 _mt5_for_duel — login range 7700000..7799999 with sum-of-ord seed has only ~100k unique values; collisions possible across many users. Acceptable for mock demo.
server.py:909 mt5 for royale uses lobby_id+user_id same scheme as duel — deterministic, but a real impl would need MT5 creds scoped per-event, which is fine for the mock.
test_credentials: Mock auth via X-Username header. Seeded users: TradeFury (demo), GoldHands, MoCapital, FXSamurai, RileyJess, StealthAlpha, PaperHandsNoMore, CryptoKing, TradeNova, MarketBeast. Admin token: Bearer admin-secret-token-2026.
seed_data_creation: Tests rely on existing seed (T-SPRING, T-BRONZE, Diamond Open 2026, February Open, 6 royale lobbies). Royale phase-math tests temporarily mutate started_at via direct pymongo and restore in finally blocks.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run all backend tests with `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. New v4.2 suite is /app/backend/tests/test_journey_and_royale.py. To verify phase-math deterministically, tests mutate started_at in mongo (MONGO_URL/DB_NAME from /app/backend/.env) and restore. Skipped tests (test_tournament_403_not_registered, test_station_royale_403) self-skip if the chosen fallback user (@PaperHandsNoMore) happens to already be registered/in the lobby — not a failure. T-SPRING bracket is deterministic: TradeFury exits at Semi-Finals with $4,843 prize and 11,641 total P&L. T-BRONZE bracket: TradeFury exits at R16. Diamond Open 2026: ongoing Group Stage (prize_won=0).
rca of the issue: No production bugs. Two initial test failures were due to two seed-data realities: (a) the '22m in' lobby was actually ~34m in due to container uptime since seed (started_at is absolute, not relative-to-now), and (b) seed declares size=20 but only 10 users are seeded so total_active is capped at 10. Fixed by having the phase-math tests force started_at to the exact desired offsets via direct pymongo (consistent with the user's note to do exactly that). All 25 new tests + all 40 matchmaking-v41 regression tests pass.
